search_console.name: list_accounts
search_console.description: List all authenticated Google accounts and their associated GSC sites.

search_console.name: list_sites
search_console.description: List all sites you have access to in Google Search Console. When multiple accounts exist and no account is specified, shows all accounts' sites grouped by account.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: query_search_analytics
search_console.description: Query Google Search Console search analytics data. Returns search queries, clicks, impressions, CTR, and average position.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

siteUrl.description: The site URL to query (e.g., "https://example.com" or "sc-domain:example.com")

rowLimit.description: Maximum number of rows to return (1-25000). Default: 1000

search_console.name: inspect_url
search_console.description: Inspect a URL to check its indexing status, mobile usability, and rich results. Shows if Google can crawl and index the page.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

inspectionUrl.description: The full URL to inspect (e.g., https://example.com/page)

search_console.name: list_sitemaps
search_console.description: List all sitemaps submitted for a site in Google Search Console.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: find_keyword_opportunities
search_console.description: Find keyword optimization opportunities - queries with high impressions but low CTR that could be improved.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

maxCtr.description: Maximum CTR to consider as opportunity (default: 0.03 = 3%)

search_console.name: get_top_pages
search_console.description: Get top performing pages sorted by clicks, impressions, CTR, or position.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: compare_performance
search_console.description: Compare search performance between two time periods. Shows which queries/pages gained or lost traffic.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: analyze_brand_queries
search_console.description: Analyze branded vs non-branded search queries. Shows what percentage of traffic comes from brand searches.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

brandTerms.description: Your brand terms to identify branded queries (e.g., ["mycompany", "my company", "mybrand"])

search_console.name: get_keyword_trend
search_console.description: Get the performance trend of a specific keyword over time. Shows daily clicks, impressions, and position changes.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: export_analytics
search_console.description: Export search analytics data as CSV or JSON format for external analysis or reporting.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

search_console.name: query_by_search_appearance
search_console.description: Query analytics filtered by search appearance type (AMP, Rich Results, Video, FAQ, etc.).

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

searchAppearance.description: The search appearance type to filter by

search_console.name: query_by_search_type
search_console.description: Query analytics filtered by search type: web, image, video, news, or discover.

account.description: Account alias to use (e.g., "default", "personal"). If omitted and only one account exists, it is used automatically.

searchType.description: The search type to filter by

google_workspace.name: start_google_auth
google_workspace.description:
Manually initiate Google OAuth authentication flow.

NOTE: This is a legacy OAuth 2.0 tool and is disabled when OAuth 2.1 is enabled.
The authentication system automatically handles credential checks and prompts for
authentication when needed. Only use this tool if:
1. You need to re-authenticate with different credentials
2. You want to proactively authenticate before using other tools
3. The automatic authentication flow failed and you need to retry

In most cases, simply try calling the Google Workspace tool you need - it will
automatically handle authentication if required.

google_workspace.name: search_gmail_messages
google_workspace.description:
Searches messages in a user's Gmail account based on a query.
Returns both Message IDs and Thread IDs for each found message, along with Gmail web interface links for manual verification.
Supports pagination via page_token parameter.

query.description: The search query. Supports standard Gmail search operators.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

page_size.description: The maximum number of messages to return. Defaults to 10.

page_token.description: Token for retrieving the next page of results. Use the next_page_token from a previous response.

google_workspace.name: get_gmail_message_content
google_workspace.description: Retrieves the full content (subject, sender, recipients, body) of a specific Gmail message.

message_id.description: The unique ID of the Gmail message to retrieve.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

body_format.description: Body output format. 'text' (default) returns plaintext (HTML converted to text as fallback). 'html' returns the raw HTML body as-is without conversion. 'raw' fetches the full raw MIME message and returns the base64url-decoded content.

google_workspace.name: get_gmail_messages_content_batch
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves the content of multiple Gmail messages in a single batch request.
Supports up to 25 messages per batch to prevent SSL connection exhaustion.

message_ids.description: List of Gmail message IDs to retrieve (max 25 per batch).

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

format.description: Message format. "full" includes body, "metadata" only headers.

body_format.description: Body output format (only applies when format='full'). 'text' (default) returns plaintext (HTML converted to text as fallback). 'html' returns the raw HTML body as-is without conversion. 'raw' fetches the full raw MIME message and returns the base64url-decoded content.

google_workspace.name: get_gmail_attachment_content
google_workspace.description:
Downloads an email attachment and saves it to local disk.

In stdio mode, returns the local file path for direct access.
In HTTP mode, returns a temporary download URL (valid for 1 hour).
May re-fetch message metadata to resolve filename and MIME type.

message_id.description: The ID of the Gmail message containing the attachment.

attachment_id.description: The ID of the attachment to download.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

return_base64.description:
When True, includes the full attachment as a
standard base64 string in the response (in addition to any file
path or download URL). Useful for sandboxed clients that cannot
reach localhost download URLs or the MCP server's local file
paths (e.g. containerized agents with network allowlists). The
returned base64 uses the standard alphabet, so it can be passed
directly to tools like ``draft_gmail_message`` that expect
standard (not URL-safe) base64. Default False preserves the
existing behavior and response size.

google_workspace.name: send_gmail_message
google_workspace.description:
Sends an email using the user's Gmail account. Supports both new emails and replies with optional attachments.
Supports Gmail's "Send As" feature to send from configured alias addresses.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required for authentication.

body.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML).

body_format.description: Email body format. Use 'plain' for plaintext or 'html' for HTML content.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name (e.g., 'Peter Hartree'). If provided, the From header will be formatted as 'Name <email>'.

from_email.description: Optional 'Send As' alias email address. Must be configured in Gmail settings (Settings > Accounts > Send mail as). If not provided, uses the authenticated user's email.

thread_id.description: Optional Gmail thread ID to reply within.

in_reply_to.description: Optional RFC Message-ID of the message being replied to (e.g., '<message123@gmail.com>').

references.description: Optional chain of Message-IDs for proper threading.

attachments.description: Optional list of attachments. Each can have: "url" (fetch from URL — works with MCP attachment URLs from get_drive_file_download_url / get_gmail_attachment_content), OR "path" (file path, auto-encodes), OR "content" (standard base64, not urlsafe) + "filename". Optional "mime_type". Example: [{"url": "https://host/attachments/abc-123", "filename": "report.pdf"}]

include_signature.description: Whether to append the Gmail signature from Settings > Signature when available. Defaults to true.

google_workspace.name: draft_gmail_message
google_workspace.description:
Creates a draft email in the user's Gmail account. Supports both new drafts and reply drafts with optional attachments.
Supports Gmail's "Send As" feature to draft from configured alias addresses.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required for authentication.

body_format.description: Email body format. Use 'plain' for plaintext or 'html' for HTML content.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name (e.g., 'Peter Hartree'). If provided, the From header will be formatted as 'Name <email>'.

from_email.description: Optional 'Send As' alias email address. Must be configured in Gmail settings (Settings > Accounts > Send mail as). If not provided, uses the authenticated user's email.

thread_id.description: Optional Gmail thread ID to reply within.

in_reply_to.description: Optional RFC Message-ID of the message being replied to (e.g., '<message123@gmail.com>').

references.description: Optional chain of Message-IDs for proper threading.

attachments.description: Optional list of attachments. Each can have: 'url' (fetch from URL — works with MCP attachment URLs from get_drive_file_download_url / get_gmail_attachment_content), OR 'path' (file path, auto-encodes), OR 'content' (standard base64, not urlsafe) + 'filename'. Optional 'mime_type' (auto-detected if not provided).

include_signature.description: Whether to append the Gmail signature from Settings > Signature when available. Defaults to true.

quote_original.description: Whether to include the original message as a quoted reply. Requires thread_id. Defaults to false.

google_workspace.name: get_gmail_thread_content
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves the complete content of a Gmail conversation thread, including all messages.

Optionally also returns structured ownership analysis so a caller can
determine who sent the last message and who owes whom a response without
re-parsing the formatted string or making a second tool call.

thread_id.description: The unique ID of the Gmail thread to retrieve.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

body_format.description: Body output format. 'text' (default) returns plaintext (HTML converted to text as fallback). 'html' returns the raw HTML body as-is without conversion. 'raw' fetches each message's full raw MIME content and returns the base64url-decoded body.

include_analysis.description: When True, the return value is a dict with both the formatted thread content AND structured ownership analysis (last sender, ball-in-court verdict, per-sender message counts, participants). Defaults to False, in which case the existing string return shape is preserved.

google_workspace.name: get_gmail_threads_content_batch
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves the content of multiple Gmail threads in a single batch request.
Supports up to 25 threads per batch to prevent SSL connection exhaustion.

thread_ids.description: A list of Gmail thread IDs to retrieve. The function will automatically batch requests in chunks of 25.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

body_format.description: Body output format. 'text' (default) returns plaintext (HTML converted to text as fallback). 'html' returns the raw HTML body as-is without conversion. 'raw' fetches each message's full raw MIME content and returns the base64url-decoded body.

google_workspace.name: list_gmail_labels
google_workspace.description: Lists all labels in the user's Gmail account.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

google_workspace.name: manage_gmail_label
google_workspace.description: Manages Gmail labels: create, update, or delete labels.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

action.description: Action to perform on the label.

name.description: Label name. Required for create, optional for update.

label_id.description: Label ID. Required for update and delete operations.

label_list_visibility.description: Whether the label is shown in the label list.

message_list_visibility.description: Whether the label is shown in the message list.

google_workspace.name: list_gmail_filters
google_workspace.description: Lists all Gmail filters configured in the user's mailbox.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

google_workspace.name: manage_gmail_filter
google_workspace.description: Manages Gmail filters. Supports creating and deleting filters.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

filter_action.description: Filter action object (required for create). Named 'filter_action' to avoid shadowing the 'action' parameter.

filter_id.description: ID of the filter to delete (required for delete).

google_workspace.name: modify_gmail_message_labels
google_workspace.description:
Adds or removes labels from a Gmail message.
To archive an email, remove the INBOX label.
To delete an email, add the TRASH label.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

message_id.description: The ID of the message to modify.

add_label_ids.description: List of label IDs to add to the message.

remove_label_ids.description: List of label IDs to remove from the message.

google_workspace.name: batch_modify_gmail_message_labels
google_workspace.description: Adds or removes labels from multiple Gmail messages in a single batch request.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

message_ids.description: A list of message IDs to modify.

add_label_ids.description: List of label IDs to add to the messages.

remove_label_ids.description: List of label IDs to remove from the messages.

google_workspace.name: list_calendars
google_workspace.description: Retrieves a list of calendars accessible to the authenticated user.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

google_workspace.name: get_events
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves events from a specified Google Calendar. Can retrieve a single event by ID or multiple events within a time range.
You can also search for events by keyword by supplying the optional "query" param.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

calendar_id.description: The ID of the calendar to query. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar. Defaults to 'primary'. Calendar IDs can be obtained using `list_calendars`.

event_id.description: The ID of a specific event to retrieve. If provided, retrieves only this event and ignores time filtering parameters.

time_min.description: The start of the time range (inclusive) in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2024-05-12T10:00:00Z' or '2024-05-12'). If omitted, defaults to the current time. Ignored if event_id is provided.

time_max.description: The end of the time range (exclusive) in RFC3339 format. If omitted, events starting from `time_min` onwards are considered (up to `max_results`). Ignored if event_id is provided.

max_results.description: The maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 25. Ignored if event_id is provided.

query.description: A keyword to search for within event fields (summary, description, location). Ignored if event_id is provided.

detailed.description: Whether to return detailed event information including description, location, attendees, and attendee details (response status, organizer, optional flags). Defaults to False.

include_attachments.description: Whether to include attachment information in detailed event output. When True, shows attachment details (fileId, fileUrl, mimeType, title) for events that have attachments. Only applies when detailed=True. Set this to True when you need to view or access files that have been attached to calendar events, such as meeting documents, presentations, or other shared files. Defaults to False.

google_workspace.name: manage_event
google_workspace.description: Manages calendar events. Supports creating, updating, deleting, and RSVP.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

start_time.description: Start time in RFC3339 format (required for create).

end_time.description: End time in RFC3339 format (required for create).

event_id.description: Event ID (required for update and delete).

attachments.description: List of Google Drive file URLs or IDs to attach.

recurrence.description: RFC5545 recurrence rules for a recurring event, e.g. ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=10"].

guests_can_see_other_guests.description: Whether attendees can see other guests.

rsvp_comment.description: Optional message to include with the RSVP response (rsvp action only).

send_updates.description: Notification behavior for create, update, delete, and rsvp — "all" (default), "externalOnly", or "none".

google_workspace.name: manage_out_of_office
google_workspace.description:
Manages Out of Office events on Google Calendar. These special events auto-decline
meeting invitations and set the user's status to "Out of office" across Google Workspace.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

start_time.description: Start date/time. Use 'YYYY-MM-DD' for full-day or RFC3339 for partial-day (e.g., '2024-04-05T09:00:00Z'). Date-only values are auto-converted to dateTime (midnight-to-midnight). Required for create.

end_time.description: End date/time (exclusive). Same format as start_time. For a single full day on April 5, use start_time='2026-04-05' and end_time='2026-04-06'. Required for create.

summary.description: Display text on the calendar. Defaults to "Out of Office".

auto_decline_mode.description: How to handle conflicting invitations. One of: "declineAllConflictingInvitations" (default), "declineOnlyNewConflictingInvitations", "declineNone".

recurrence.description: RFC5545 recurrence rules for a recurring Out of Office series, e.g. ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=10"].

timezone.description: Timezone for the event (e.g., "America/New_York", "Europe/London"). Required when using date-only values or dateTime values without an explicit UTC offset.

time_min.description: For "list" action: start of time range. Defaults to current time. Recurring series are expanded into individual instances in the requested range.

time_max.description: For "list" action: end of time range.

max_results.description: For "list" action: maximum events to return. Defaults to 10.

event_id.description: Event ID. Required for "update" and "delete" actions.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID. Defaults to 'primary'. Out of Office status events live on primary calendars, so use 'primary' or a user's primary calendar ID/email rather than a secondary calendar ID.

google_workspace.name: manage_focus_time
google_workspace.description:
Manages Focus Time events on Google Calendar. These special events auto-decline
meeting invitations and, by default, set the user's chat status to Do Not
Disturb, helping protect blocks of uninterrupted work time.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

start_time.description: Start date/time. Use 'YYYY-MM-DD' for full-day or RFC3339 for partial-day (e.g., '2024-04-05T09:00:00Z'). Date-only values are auto-converted to dateTime (midnight-to-midnight). Required for create.

end_time.description: End date/time (exclusive). Same format as start_time. For a single full day on April 5, use start_time='2026-04-05' and end_time='2026-04-06'. Required for create.

summary.description: Display text on the calendar. Defaults to "Focus Time".

description.description: Event description. Useful for adding context about what the focus time is for.

auto_decline_mode.description: How to handle conflicting invitations. One of: "declineAllConflictingInvitations" (default), "declineOnlyNewConflictingInvitations", "declineNone".

chat_status.description: Google Chat status during the focus time. Supports "doNotDisturb" (default) and "available".

recurrence.description: RFC5545 recurrence rules for a recurring Focus Time series, e.g. ["RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=10"].

timezone.description: Timezone for the event (e.g., "America/New_York", "Europe/London"). Required when using date-only values or dateTime values without an explicit UTC offset.

time_min.description: For "list" action: start of time range. Defaults to current time. Recurring series are expanded into individual instances in the requested range.

time_max.description: For "list" action: end of time range.

max_results.description: For "list" action: maximum events to return. Defaults to 10.

event_id.description: Event ID. Required for "update" and "delete" actions.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID. Defaults to 'primary'. Focus Time status events live on primary calendars, so use 'primary' or a user's primary calendar ID/email rather than a secondary calendar ID.

google_workspace.name: query_freebusy
google_workspace.description: Returns free/busy information for a set of calendars.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

time_min.description: The start of the interval for the query in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2024-05-12T10:00:00Z' or '2024-05-12').

time_max.description: The end of the interval for the query in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2024-05-12T18:00:00Z' or '2024-05-12').

calendar_ids.description: List of calendar identifiers to query. If not provided, queries the primary calendar. Use 'primary' for the user's primary calendar or specific calendar IDs obtained from `list_calendars`.

group_expansion_max.description: Maximum number of calendar identifiers to be provided for a single group. Optional. An error is returned for a group with more members than this value. Maximum value is 100.

calendar_expansion_max.description: Maximum number of calendars for which FreeBusy information is to be provided. Optional. Maximum value is 50.

google_workspace.name: create_calendar
google_workspace.description: Creates a new secondary Google Calendar.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

summary.description: The title/name of the new calendar.

description.description: An optional description for the calendar.

google_workspace.name: search_drive_files
google_workspace.description: Searches for files and folders within a user's Google Drive, including shared drives.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

query.description:
The search query string. Supports Google Drive search operators.
         NOTE: Owner-based queries ('user@example.com' in owners) DO NOT WORK in Shared Drives
         because files are owned by the shared drive itself, not individual users.
         For recent files by a specific user in Shared Drives, search by modifiedTime
         and use order_by='modifiedTime desc' instead.

page_size.description: The maximum number of files to return. Defaults to 10.

page_token.description: Page token from a previous response's nextPageToken to retrieve the next page of results.

drive_id.description: ID of the shared drive to search. If None, behavior depends on `corpora` and `include_items_from_all_drives`.

include_items_from_all_drives.description: Whether shared drive items should be included in results. Defaults to True. This is effective when not specifying a `drive_id`.

corpora.description:
Bodies of items to query (e.g., 'user', 'domain', 'drive', 'allDrives').
                     If 'drive_id' is specified and 'corpora' is None, it defaults to 'drive'.
                     Otherwise, Drive API default behavior applies. Prefer 'user' or 'drive' over 'allDrives' for efficiency.

file_type.description:
Restrict results to a specific file type. Accepts a friendly
                       name ('folder', 'document'/'doc', 'spreadsheet'/'sheet',
                       'presentation'/'slides', 'form', 'drawing', 'pdf', 'shortcut',
                       'script', 'site', 'jam'/'jamboard') or any raw MIME type
                       string (e.g. 'application/pdf'). Defaults to None (all types).

detailed.description: Whether to include size, modified time, and link in results. Defaults to True.

order_by.description:
Sort order. Comma-separated list of sort keys with optional 'desc' modifier.
                      Valid keys: 'createdTime', 'folder', 'modifiedByMeTime', 'modifiedTime',
                      'name', 'name_natural', 'quotaBytesUsed', 'recency', 'sharedWithMeTime',
                      'starred', 'viewedByMeTime'. Example: 'modifiedTime desc' or 'folder,modifiedTime desc,name'.
                      Defaults to None (Drive API default ordering).

google_workspace.name: get_drive_file_content
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves the content of a specific Google Drive file by ID, supporting files in shared drives.

• Native Google Docs, Sheets, Slides → exported as text / CSV.
• Office files (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx) → unzipped & parsed with std-lib to
  extract readable text.
• PDFs → text extracted with pypdf when possible; scanned/image-only PDFs
  fall back to a download hint.
• Images → returned as base64 with MIME metadata for multimodal clients.
• Any other file → downloaded; tries UTF-8 decode, else notes binary.

user_google_email.description: The user’s Google email address.

google_workspace.name: get_drive_file_download_url
google_workspace.description:
Downloads a Google Drive file and saves it to local disk.

In stdio mode, returns the local file path for direct access.
In HTTP mode, returns a temporary download URL (valid for 1 hour).

For Google native files (Docs, Sheets, Slides), exports to a useful format:
- Google Docs -> PDF (default) or DOCX if export_format='docx'
- Google Sheets -> XLSX (default), PDF if export_format='pdf', or CSV if export_format='csv'
- Google Slides -> PDF (default) or PPTX if export_format='pptx'

For other files, downloads the original file format.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The Google Drive file ID to download.

export_format.description:
Optional export format for Google native files.
          Options: 'pdf', 'docx', 'xlsx', 'csv', 'pptx'.
          If not specified, uses sensible defaults (PDF for Docs/Slides, XLSX for Sheets).
          For Sheets: supports 'csv', 'pdf', or 'xlsx' (default).

google_workspace.name: list_drive_items
google_workspace.description:
Lists files/folders or shared drive containers, supporting shared drives.
If `drive_id` is specified, lists items within that shared drive. `folder_id` is then relative to that drive (or use drive_id as folder_id for root).
If `drive_id` is not specified, lists items from user's "My Drive" and accessible shared drives (if `include_items_from_all_drives` is True).
Set `resource_type` to "shared_drives" to list shared drive containers instead of folder contents.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

folder_id.description: The ID of the Google Drive folder. Defaults to 'root'. For a shared drive, this can be the shared drive's ID to list its root, or a folder ID within that shared drive.

page_size.description: The maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100.

page_token.description: Page token from a previous response's nextPageToken to retrieve the next page of results.

drive_id.description: ID of the shared drive. If provided, the listing is scoped to this drive.

include_items_from_all_drives.description: Whether items from all accessible shared drives should be included if `drive_id` is not set. Defaults to True.

corpora.description: Corpus to query ('user', 'drive', 'allDrives'). If `drive_id` is set and `corpora` is None, 'drive' is used. If None and no `drive_id`, API defaults apply.

file_type.description:
Restrict results to a specific file type. Accepts a friendly
                       name ('folder', 'document'/'doc', 'spreadsheet'/'sheet',
                       'presentation'/'slides', 'form', 'drawing', 'pdf', 'shortcut',
                       'script', 'site', 'jam'/'jamboard') or any raw MIME type
                       string (e.g. 'application/pdf'). Defaults to None (all types).

detailed.description: Whether to include size, modified time, and link in results. Defaults to True.

order_by.description:
Sort order. Comma-separated list of sort keys with optional 'desc' modifier.
                      Valid keys: 'createdTime', 'folder', 'modifiedByMeTime', 'modifiedTime',
                      'name', 'name_natural', 'quotaBytesUsed', 'recency', 'sharedWithMeTime',
                      'starred', 'viewedByMeTime'. Example: 'modifiedTime desc' or 'folder,modifiedTime desc,name'.
                      Defaults to None (Drive API default ordering).

resource_type.description:
What to list. Use "items" for folder contents or
                 "shared_drives" for shared drive containers. Defaults to "items".

query.description:
Shared drive query used only when resource_type="shared_drives",
                   e.g. "name contains 'Engineering'".

include_organizers.description:
When resource_type="shared_drives", include principals
                       with the organizer role. This costs one extra permissions.list
                       API call per shared drive returned. Defaults to False.

google_workspace.name: create_drive_folder
google_workspace.description: Creates a new folder in Google Drive, supporting creation within shared drives.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

folder_name.description: The name for the new folder.

parent_folder_id.description:
The ID of the parent folder. Defaults to 'root'.
For shared drives, use a folder ID within that shared drive.

google_workspace.name: create_drive_file
google_workspace.description:
Creates a new file in Google Drive, supporting creation within shared drives.
Accepts either direct content or a fileUrl to fetch the content from.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_name.description: The name for the new file.

content.description: If provided, the content to write to the file.

folder_id.description: The ID of the parent folder. Defaults to 'root'. For shared drives, this must be a folder ID within the shared drive.

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the file. Defaults to 'text/plain'.

fileUrl.description: If provided, fetches the file content from this URL. Supports file://, http://, and https:// protocols.

google_workspace.name: import_to_google_doc
google_workspace.description:
Imports a file (Markdown, DOCX, TXT, HTML, RTF, ODT) into Google Docs format with automatic conversion.

Google Drive automatically converts the source file to native Google Docs format,
preserving formatting like headings, lists, bold, italic, etc.
For batch operations, prefer file_path for files on disk so callers do not need
to load full file contents into their context.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_name.description: The name for the new Google Doc (extension will be ignored).

content.description: Text content for text-based formats. Use only for short snippets or content already in memory.

file_path.description: Local file path or file:// URL for any supported format (MD, TXT, HTML, DOCX, ODT, RTF). Appropriate for larger files than content, but file_path may still load the file into memory or perform non-streaming reads. Avoid very large files that could exceed memory or time limits; use streaming/chunked uploads or an alternative API for huge files.

file_url.description: Remote URL to fetch the file from (http/https).

source_format.description:
Source format hint ('md', 'markdown', 'docx', 'txt', 'html', 'rtf', 'odt').
                           Auto-detected from file_name extension if not provided.

folder_id.description: The ID of the parent folder. Defaults to 'root'.

google_workspace.name: get_drive_file_permissions
google_workspace.description:
Gets detailed metadata about a Google Drive file including sharing permissions,
parent folder IDs, ownership, and lifecycle timestamps.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to check permissions for.

google_workspace.name: check_drive_file_public_access
google_workspace.description: Searches for a file by name and checks if it has public link sharing enabled.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_name.description: The name of the file to check.

drive_id.description:
ID of the shared drive to scope the search to. When set, the
                      underlying files.list call uses corpora='drive' and the given
                      driveId, which is required to reliably find files that live only
                      in that shared drive. When None, behaviour is unchanged
                      (default API corpora applies).

google_workspace.name: update_drive_file
google_workspace.description: Updates metadata and properties of a Google Drive file.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to update. Required.

mime_type.description: New MIME type (note: changing type may require content upload).

add_parents.description: Comma-separated folder IDs to add as parents.

remove_parents.description: Comma-separated folder IDs to remove from parents.

trashed.description: Whether to move file to/from trash.

writers_can_share.description: Whether editors can share the file.

properties.description: Custom key-value properties for the file.

google_workspace.name: get_drive_shareable_link
google_workspace.description: Gets the shareable link for a Google Drive file or folder.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file or folder to get the shareable link for. Required.

google_workspace.name: manage_drive_access
google_workspace.description:
Consolidated tool for managing Google Drive file and folder access permissions.

Supports granting, batch-granting, updating, revoking permissions, and
transferring file ownership -- all through a single entry point.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file or folder. Required.

action.description:
The access management action to perform. Required. One of:
- "grant": Share with a single user, group, domain, or anyone.
- "grant_batch": Share with multiple recipients in one call.
- "update": Modify an existing permission (role or expiration).
- "revoke": Remove an existing permission.
- "transfer_owner": Transfer file ownership to another user.

share_with.description:
Email address (user/group), domain name (domain),
or omit for 'anyone'. Used by "grant".

role.description:
Permission role -- 'reader', 'commenter', or 'writer'.
Used by "grant" (defaults to 'reader') and "update".

share_type.description:
Type of sharing -- 'user', 'group', 'domain', or 'anyone'.
Used by "grant". Defaults to 'user'.

permission_id.description:
The permission ID to modify or remove.
Required for "update" and "revoke" actions.

recipients.description:
List of recipient objects for
"grant_batch". Each should have: email (str), role (str, optional),
share_type (str, optional), expiration_time (str, optional). For domain
shares use 'domain' field instead of 'email'.

send_notification.description:
Whether to send notification emails. Defaults to True.
Used by "grant" and "grant_batch".

email_message.description:
Custom notification email message.
Used by "grant" and "grant_batch".

expiration_time.description:
Expiration in RFC 3339 format
(e.g., "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z"). Used by "grant" and "update".

allow_file_discovery.description:
For 'domain'/'anyone' shares, whether
the file appears in search. Used by "grant".

new_owner_email.description:
Email of the new owner.
Required for "transfer_owner".

move_to_new_owners_root.description:
Move file to the new owner's My Drive root.
Defaults to False. Used by "transfer_owner".

google_workspace.name: copy_drive_file
google_workspace.description:
Creates a copy of an existing Google Drive file.

This tool copies the template document to a new location with an optional new name.
The copy maintains all formatting and content from the original file.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to copy. Required.

new_name.description: New name for the copied file. If not provided, uses "Copy of [original name]".

parent_folder_id.description: The ID of the folder where the copy should be created. Defaults to 'root' (My Drive).

google_workspace.name: set_drive_file_permissions
google_workspace.description:
Sets file-level sharing settings and controls link sharing for a Google Drive file or folder.

This is a high-level tool for the most common permission changes. Use this to toggle
"anyone with the link" access or configure file-level sharing behavior. For managing
individual user/group permissions, use share_drive_file or update_drive_permission instead.

user_google_email.description: The user's Google email address. Required.

file_id.description: The ID of the file or folder. Required.

link_sharing.description:
Control "anyone with the link" access for the file.
- "off": Disable "anyone with the link" access for this file.
- "reader": Anyone with the link can view.
- "commenter": Anyone with the link can comment.
- "writer": Anyone with the link can edit.

writers_can_share.description:
Whether editors can change permissions and share.
If False, only the owner can share. Defaults to None (no change).

copy_requires_writer_permission.description:
Whether viewers and commenters
are prevented from copying, printing, or downloading. Defaults to None (no change).

google_workspace.name: search_docs
google_workspace.description:
Searches for Google Docs by name using Drive API (mimeType filter).

Returns:
    str: A formatted list of Google Docs matching the search query.

google_workspace.name: get_doc_content
google_workspace.description:
Retrieves content of a Google Doc or a Drive file (like .docx) identified by document_id.
- Native Google Docs: Fetches content via Docs API.
- Office files (.docx, etc.) stored in Drive: Downloads via Drive API and extracts text.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the Google Doc (or full URL)

suggestions_view_mode.description:
How to render suggestions in the returned content:
- "DEFAULT_FOR_CURRENT_ACCESS": Default based on user's access level
- "SUGGESTIONS_INLINE": Suggested changes appear inline in the document
- "PREVIEW_SUGGESTIONS_ACCEPTED": Preview as if all suggestions were accepted
- "PREVIEW_WITHOUT_SUGGESTIONS": Preview as if all suggestions were rejected

google_workspace.name: list_docs_in_folder
google_workspace.description:
Lists Google Docs within a specific Drive folder.

Returns:
    str: A formatted list of Google Docs in the specified folder.

google_workspace.name: create_doc
google_workspace.description:
Creates a new Google Doc and optionally inserts initial content.

After creation, the document body starts at index 1. A new empty doc
has total length 2 (one section break at index 0, one newline at index 1).

To build a rich document after creation, use batch_update_doc with
insert_text operations using end_of_segment=true to append content
sequentially without calculating indices. Then call inspect_doc_structure
to get exact positions before applying formatting in a separate batch call.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

content.description: Optional initial plain text content to insert

google_workspace.name: modify_doc_text
google_workspace.description:
Modifies text in a Google Doc - can insert/replace text and/or apply formatting in a single operation.

TIP: To append text to the end of the document without calculating indices,
set end_of_segment=true. This avoids index calculation errors.

For ordinary header/footer text, prefer update_doc_headers_footers.
Only pass segment_id when you already have a real header/footer/footnote
segment ID from inspect_doc_structure output. Do not guess IDs such as
"kix.header" or "kix.footer".

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

start_index.description:
Start position for operation using Docs API indices from
inspect_doc_structure. For the main body, 0 is also accepted as an
alias for the first writable position.

end_index.description: End position for text replacement/formatting (if not provided with text, text is inserted)

text.description: New text to insert or replace with (optional - can format existing text without changing it)

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID to target

end_of_segment.description: Insert text at the end of the targeted segment instead of start_index

bold.description: Whether to make text bold (True/False/None to leave unchanged)

italic.description: Whether to make text italic (True/False/None to leave unchanged)

underline.description: Whether to underline text (True/False/None to leave unchanged)

strikethrough.description: Whether to strike through text (True/False/None to leave unchanged)

font_weight.description: Font weight (100-900 in steps of 100; requires font_family)

clear_link.description: Remove hyperlink from the target range

google_workspace.name: find_and_replace_doc
google_workspace.description:
Finds and replaces text throughout a Google Doc. No index calculation required.

This is the safest way to update specific text in a document because it
does not require knowing any indices. Use this tool when you need to:
- Replace placeholder text (e.g., {{TITLE}}) with real content
- Update specific words or phrases throughout the document
- Make targeted text changes without risk of index errors

For building documents from scratch, consider inserting text with unique
placeholders via batch_update_doc, then using this tool to replace them.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

tab_id.description: Optional ID of the tab to target

google_workspace.name: insert_doc_elements
google_workspace.description: Inserts structural elements like tables, lists, or page breaks into a Google Doc.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

rows.description: Number of rows for table (required for table)

columns.description: Number of columns for table (required for table)

text.description: Initial text content for list items

google_workspace.name: insert_doc_image
google_workspace.description: Inserts an image into a Google Doc from Drive or a URL.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

image_source.description: Drive file ID or public image URL

google_workspace.name: update_doc_headers_footers
google_workspace.description:
Safely creates or updates header/footer text in a Google Doc.

This is the default tool for header/footer content. Do NOT use
batch_update_doc with create_header_footer just to set header/footer text;
that low-level operation is only for advanced section-break workflows and
can fail when the default header/footer already exists.

This tool handles both creation and update in one call:
- If the header/footer does not exist, it is automatically created first.
- If the header/footer already exists, its content is replaced.

You do NOT need to create a header/footer separately before calling this tool.
Simply call it with the desired content and it will work whether the
header/footer exists or not.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

section_type.description: Type of section to create or update ("header" or "footer")

google_workspace.name: batch_update_doc
google_workspace.description:
Executes multiple low-level document operations in a single atomic batch update.

For normal header/footer text, prefer update_doc_headers_footers.
Only use create_header_footer here for advanced section-break layouts.

RECOMMENDED WORKFLOW FOR BUILDING DOCUMENTS:
=============================================
To avoid index calculation errors, build documents in phases:

PHASE 1 - INSERT ALL CONTENT (use end_of_segment=true, no index math):
  Append text, section breaks, and page breaks sequentially.
  Each operation appends to the end of the body. No index needed.
  Example batch: [
    {"type": "insert_text", "end_of_segment": true, "text": "Report Title\n"},
    {"type": "insert_text", "end_of_segment": true, "text": "\nExecutive Summary\n"},
    {"type": "insert_text", "end_of_segment": true, "text": "Revenue grew 15%.\n"},
    {"type": "insert_section_break", "end_of_segment": true, "section_type": "NEXT_PAGE"},
    {"type": "insert_text", "end_of_segment": true, "text": "Detailed Analysis\n"}
  ]

PHASE 2 - CREATE HEADERS/FOOTERS (if needed):
  For normal header/footer text, use update_doc_headers_footers
  (it auto-creates if missing and writes the content for you).
  Only include create_header_footer operations in a batch when you are
  intentionally managing advanced section-break-specific layouts.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

end_of_segment.description: Append to the end of the targeted body/segment instead of using index.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

end_of_segment.description: Append to the end of the targeted body/segment instead of using index.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.

end_of_segment.description: Append to the end of the body instead of using index.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

end_of_segment.description: Append to the end of the body instead of using index.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.
Optional document tab ID to target.

section_break_index.description: Optional section break index for section-scoped layouts.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target.

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID. Use a real ID returned by inspect_doc_structure; do not guess values like 'kix.header'.

image_uri.description: Image URL or resolvable image URI.

end_of_segment.description: Append to the end of the targeted body/segment instead of using index.

operations.description:
List of operation dicts. Each operation MUST have a 'type' field.
        All operations accept an optional 'tab_id' to target a specific tab.

google_workspace.name: inspect_doc_structure
google_workspace.description:
Essential tool for finding safe insertion points and understanding document structure.

USE THIS FOR:
- Finding the correct index for table insertion
- Understanding document layout before making changes
- Locating existing tables and their positions
- Getting document statistics and complexity info
- Inspecting structure of specific tabs

CRITICAL FOR TABLE OPERATIONS:
ALWAYS call this BEFORE creating tables to get a safe insertion index.

WHAT THE OUTPUT SHOWS:
- total_elements: Number of document elements
- total_length: Maximum safe index for insertion
- tables: Number of existing tables
- table_details: Position and dimensions of each table
- headers / footers: Real segment IDs and previews for header/footer editing
- tabs: List of available tabs in the document (if no tab_id specified)

WORKFLOW FOR TABLE INSERTION:
Step 1: Call this function
Step 2: Note the "total_length" value
Step 3: Use an index < total_length for table insertion
Step 4: Create your table

FORMATTING WORKFLOW:
After inserting all text via batch_update_doc with end_of_segment=true,
call this tool with detailed=true to get exact start_index and end_index
for every paragraph. Use those indices directly in format_text and
update_paragraph_style operations in a second batch_update_doc call.

HEADER/FOOTER WORKFLOW:
For ordinary header/footer text, use update_doc_headers_footers.
If you need low-level segment editing, call this tool first and use the
real segment_id values returned under headers/footers. Do not invent IDs.

The detailed output includes elements[].start_index and elements[].end_index
with text_preview for each paragraph, making it easy to identify which
ranges to format.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to inspect

detailed.description: Whether to return detailed structure information

tab_id.description: Optional ID of the tab to inspect. If not provided, inspects main document.

google_workspace.name: debug_docs_runtime_info
google_workspace.description:
Return runtime/source information for diagnosing stale MCP server instances.

This is a temporary diagnostic tool intended to verify which code checkout
the running MCP server has loaded.

google_workspace.name: create_table_with_data
google_workspace.description:
Creates a table and populates it with data in one reliable operation.

CRITICAL: YOU MUST CALL inspect_doc_structure FIRST TO GET THE INDEX!

MANDATORY WORKFLOW - DO THESE STEPS IN ORDER:

Step 1: ALWAYS call inspect_doc_structure first
Step 2: Use the 'total_length' value from inspect_doc_structure as your index
Step 3: Format data as 2D list: [["col1", "col2"], ["row1col1", "row1col2"]]
Step 4: Call this function with the correct index and data

EXAMPLE DATA FORMAT:
table_data = [
    ["Header1", "Header2", "Header3"],    # Row 0 - headers
    ["Data1", "Data2", "Data3"],          # Row 1 - first data row
    ["Data4", "Data5", "Data6"]           # Row 2 - second data row
]

CRITICAL INDEX REQUIREMENTS:
- NEVER use index values like 1, 2, 10 without calling inspect_doc_structure first
- ALWAYS get index from inspect_doc_structure 'total_length' field
- Index must be a valid insertion point in the document

DATA FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
- Must be 2D list of strings only
- Each inner list = one table row
- All rows MUST have same number of columns
- Use empty strings "" for empty cells, never None
- Use debug_table_structure after creation to verify results

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to update

bold_headers.description: Whether to make first row bold (default: true)

tab_id.description: Optional tab ID to create the table in a specific tab

google_workspace.name: debug_table_structure
google_workspace.description:
ESSENTIAL DEBUGGING TOOL - Use this whenever tables don't work as expected.

USE THIS IMMEDIATELY WHEN:
- Table population put data in wrong cells
- You get "table not found" errors
- Data appears concatenated in first cell
- Need to understand existing table structure
- Planning to use populate_existing_table

WHAT THIS SHOWS YOU:
- Exact table dimensions (rows × columns)
- Each cell's position coordinates (row,col)
- Current content in each cell
- Insertion indices for each cell
- Table boundaries and ranges

HOW TO READ THE OUTPUT:
- "dimensions": "2x3" = 2 rows, 3 columns
- "position": "(0,0)" = first row, first column
- "current_content": What's actually in each cell right now
- "insertion_index": Where new text would be inserted in that cell

WORKFLOW INTEGRATION:
1. After creating table → Use this to verify structure
2. Before populating → Use this to plan your data format
3. After population fails → Use this to see what went wrong
4. When debugging → Compare your data array to actual table structure

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the document to inspect

table_index.description: Which table to debug (0 = first table, 1 = second table, etc.)

google_workspace.name: export_doc_to_pdf
google_workspace.description: Exports a Google Doc to PDF format and saves it to Google Drive.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the Google Doc to export

pdf_filename.description: Name for the PDF file (optional - if not provided, uses original name + "_PDF")

folder_id.description: Drive folder ID to save PDF in (optional - if not provided, saves in root)

google_workspace.name: update_paragraph_style
google_workspace.description:
Apply paragraph-level formatting, heading styles, and/or list formatting to a range in a Google Doc.

This tool can apply named heading styles (H1-H6) for semantic document structure,
create bulleted or numbered lists with nested indentation, and customize paragraph
properties like alignment, spacing, and indentation. All operations can be applied
in a single call.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

start_index.description:
Start position using Docs API indices from
inspect_doc_structure. For the main body, 0 is also accepted as an
alias for the first writable position.

end_index.description: End position (exclusive) - should cover the entire paragraph

heading_level.description:
Heading level 0-6 (0 = NORMAL_TEXT, 1 = H1, 2 = H2, etc.)
           Use for semantic document structure

indent_first_line.description: First line indent in points (e.g., 36 for 0.5 inch)

space_above.description: Space above paragraph in points (e.g., 12 for one line)

named_style_type.description:
Direct named style type - 'NORMAL_TEXT', 'TITLE', 'SUBTITLE',
             'HEADING_1' through 'HEADING_6'. Mutually exclusive with heading_level.

tab_id.description: Optional document tab ID to target

segment_id.description: Optional header/footer/footnote segment ID to target

keep_lines_together.description: Keep all lines of the paragraph together

keep_with_next.description: Keep the paragraph with the next paragraph

page_break_before.description: Start the paragraph on a new page

list_type.description: Create a list from existing paragraphs ('UNORDERED' for bullets, 'ORDERED' for numbers, 'CHECKBOX' for checklists)

list_nesting_level.description:
Nesting level for lists (0-8, where 0 is top level, default is 0)
               Use higher levels for nested/indented list items

bullet_preset.description: Optional explicit Google Docs bullet preset

google_workspace.name: get_doc_as_markdown
google_workspace.description:
Reads a Google Doc and returns it as clean Markdown with optional comment context.

Unlike get_doc_content which returns plain text, this tool preserves document
formatting as Markdown: headings, bold/italic/strikethrough, links, code spans,
ordered/unordered lists with nesting, and tables.

When comments are included (the default), each comment's anchor text — the specific
text the comment was attached to — is preserved, giving full context for the discussion.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

document_id.description: ID of the Google Doc (or full URL)

comment_mode.description:
How to display comments:
- "inline": Footnote-style references placed at the anchor text location (default)
- "appendix": All comments grouped at the bottom with blockquoted anchor text
- "none": No comments included

include_resolved.description: Whether to include resolved comments (default: False)

suggestions_view_mode.description:
How to render suggestions in the returned content:
- "DEFAULT_FOR_CURRENT_ACCESS": Default based on user's access level
- "SUGGESTIONS_INLINE": Suggested changes appear inline in the document
- "PREVIEW_SUGGESTIONS_ACCEPTED": Preview as if all suggestions were accepted
- "PREVIEW_WITHOUT_SUGGESTIONS": Preview as if all suggestions were rejected

google_workspace.name: manage_doc_tab
google_workspace.description: Manage document tabs: create, rename, delete, or populate from Markdown.

user_google_email.description: User's Google email address

tab_id.description: Tab ID (required for rename, delete, populate_from_markdown; use inspect_doc_structure to find IDs)

title.description: Tab title (required for create; used by rename)

index.description: Position index for new tab, 0-based among siblings (required for create)

parent_tab_id.description: Optional parent tab ID to nest under (create only)

replace_existing.description: Clear tab body before inserting markdown (default True)

google_workspace.name: list_document_comments
google_workspace.description: List all comments from a Google Document.

google_workspace.name: manage_document_comment
google_workspace.description:
Manage comments on a Google Document.

Actions:
  - create: Create a new document-level comment. Requires comment_content.
    Note: The Drive API cannot anchor comments to specific text; only
    the Google Docs UI can do that.
  - reply: Reply to a comment. Requires comment_id and comment_content.
  - resolve: Resolve a comment. Requires comment_id.

gemini_web_researcher.name: add_prompts_to_queue
gemini_web_researcher.description:
Adds a list of prompts to the background Deep Research queue.
The agent will process these in the background (3 at a time) using Gemini Chrome Pro.
Results will be saved in 00_Master_Brain/Deep_Research_Results.

gemini_web_researcher.name: check_queue_status
gemini_web_researcher.description: Returns the current status of the background deep research queue.

youtube_researcher.name: get_video_metadata
youtube_researcher.description:
Extracts deep backend metadata from a YouTube video including viral tags, upload date, views, likes, and full description.
Args:
    url: The full YouTube video URL.

youtube_researcher.name: get_video_transcript
youtube_researcher.description:
Extracts the full spoken transcript/script of a YouTube video so the AI can analyze the hook, pacing, and how they wrote it.
Args:
    video_id: The 11-character YouTube video ID (e.g., dQw4w9WgXcQ).

content_engine.name: empire_status
content_engine.description:
Returns a complete status report of all 3 Keystone YouTube channels
including video counts, privacy breakdowns, and last upload dates.

content_engine.name: seo_keyword_audit
content_engine.description:
Audits tags/keywords across all videos on a channel (or all channels).
Returns which master keywords are present, missing, and tag frequency analysis.
Args:
    channel: 'possibilities', 'protocols', 'oac', or 'all'

content_engine.name: sync_cross_links
content_engine.description:
Ensures all videos on a channel have the standard cross-channel link footer
in their descriptions. Appends the footer if missing.
Args:
    channel: 'possibilities', 'protocols', or 'oac'
    dry_run: If True, shows what would change without making changes.

content_engine.name: inject_master_tags
content_engine.description:
Ensures all videos on a channel have the master keyword set in their tags.
Merges with existing tags (doesn't replace).
Args:
    channel: 'possibilities', 'protocols', or 'oac'
    dry_run: If True, previews without making changes.

content_engine.name: content_calendar
content_engine.description:
Generates a content calendar for the next N weeks based on the publishing schedule:
- Protocols: Tuesday & Thursday 5:30 AM PT
- Possibilities: Monday 6:00 AM PT
- OAC: Friday (music releases)
Shows what's due, what's published, and gaps.
Args:
    weeks_ahead: Number of weeks to plan (default 4)

content_engine.name: notebooklm_prep
content_engine.description:
Packages all Research Archives and Master Brain docs into a manifest
for upload to Google NotebookLM. Returns file paths and sizes.

content_engine.name: channel_analytics
content_engine.description:
Pulls view counts, engagement metrics, and top-performing videos for a channel.
Args:
    channel: 'possibilities', 'protocols', 'oac', or 'all'

content_engine.name: generate_video_package
content_engine.description:
Generates a complete video upload package: optimized title options, description
with cross-links, tags from master keyword set + custom, and scheduling recommendation.
Args:
    channel: 'possibilities', 'protocols', or 'oac'
    topic: The video topic/subject
    target_keywords: Optional additional keywords to include

music_production.name: scan_desktop_new_songs
music_production.description:
Scans the C:\Users\Curtis\Desktop\New Songs folder for newly generated tracks from Suno.
Returns a JSON-formatted list of found audio files.

music_production.name: curate_desktop_song
music_production.description:
Curates a track in 'New Songs'. If take_song is True, moves it to 'sounds'.
If False, moves it to 'sounds/rejected' so Wayne's workspace stays clean.

music_production.name: prepare_for_toolost
music_production.description:
Packages a curated track from the 'sounds' folder into the 'ready for toolost' folder.
Generates a standardized metadata JSON package and associates it with the audio file.

music_production.name: distribute_and_sync_musixmatch
music_production.description:
Promotes files from 'ready for toolost' to 'musicmacth' for active lyrics synchronization.
Generates a standardized Musixmatch-ready .txt lyric sheet.

music_production.name: finalize_completed_album
music_production.description:
Archives a completed release from 'musicmacth' to 'compeleted albums'.
Moves all associated files (audio, metadata JSON, lyrics sheet) to final storage.

music_production.name: generate_deep_house_track
music_production.description:
Simulates calling the Suno AI API to generate a motivational Melodic Deep House background audio file.
Saves metadata to the local Tracks database folder.

music_production.name: upload_musixmatch_lyrics
music_production.description: Uploads a sync-ready lyrics sheet to the Musixmatch API for global syndication.

music_production.name: package_toolost_release
music_production.description: Packages the track audio and corresponding artwork into a digital release package matching TooLost distribution criteria.

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_ccb_optimum_calculator_calculate_bc_family_benefit
dynamic_skills.description: Calculates BC Family Benefit (BCFB) based on AFNI.

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_ccb_optimum_calculator_calculate_ccb
dynamic_skills.description:
Calculates Canada Child Benefit (CCB) based on Adjusted Family Net Income (AFNI).
Rates reflect the 2025/2026 benefit year.

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_ccb_optimum_calculator_calculate_personal_tax_bc
dynamic_skills.description:
Calculates BC and Federal Personal Income Tax, taking into account
Basic Personal Amounts and Dividend Tax Credits for non-eligible dividends.

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_ccb_optimum_calculator_model_scenario
dynamic_skills.description: Models the complete tax and benefit ecosystem for a specific net cash transfer.

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_seo_auditor_validate_sauna_pages
dynamic_skills.description:
Scans and audits all local Suna Spa HTML landing pages for:
1. Single H1 tag integrity
2. Valid embedded JSON-LD Structured Data
3. Proper link mapping to Wayne Stevenson Person ID
4. Compliance with BC General Contractor License display rules

dynamic_skills.name: dynamic_tax_utils_mock_calculate_dividend_tax_benefit
dynamic_skills.description:
Calculates non-eligible dividend vs salary split tax benefits in British Columbia for 2026.
A mock analytical tool to optimize the CCB (Canada Child Benefit) sweet spot.
